Ntc Of Motion Of Defnt Errol Robert Kolen For Stay Of Entire Action As To Errol Robert Kolen Or In The Alternative For An Extension Of Stay As To Discovery
Off Calendar Feb-02-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lauren E. Tate, Esq., Tate & Associates (925) 210-2000 for Defendant and Moving Party Kolen
Alan M. Caplan, Esq., Bushnell, Caplan & Fielding LLP (415) 217-3800, for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Rothman
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling as modified: DEFENDANT ERROL KOLEN'S Motion Of Defendant Errol Robert Kolen For Stay Of Entire Action As To Errol Robert Kolen, Or In The Alternative, For An Extension Of Stay As To Discovery: REQUEST TO STAY ENTIRE ACTION IS DENIED. PROTECTIVE ORDER IS EXTENDED AS TO DR. KOLEN UNTIL AUGUST 2, 2004; however, to the extent that Dr. Kolen is subject to an order or voluntarily makes public any documents, the privilege against self incrimination is waived. DR. KOLEN'S OBLIGATION TO FILE A VERIFIED ANSWER IS ALSO STAYED UNTIL AUGUST 2, 2004. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Re: 4th c/a - sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead dangerous condition of property that contributed to plaintiff's injuries. Re: c/a 5, 6, 8, 9 - overruled. Re: 7th c/a - sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead sexually offensive contact if can do so in good faith.
